title: Clitandra togolana
intro: Clitandra togolana is a tropical plant species belonging to the Clitandra genus, native to West Africa, and known for its medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as its ability to regulate blood sugar levels and exhibit antioxidant activity. The bioactive constituents of Clitandra togolana include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Clitandra togolana is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections.
taxonomy: Clitandra togolana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This plant is a member of the genus Clitandra, which is a group of flowering plants native to tropical Africa. Clitandra togolana is further classified as a species within the Clitandra genus, and is native to Togo in West Africa. The plant's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other species within the Apocynaceae family. Clitandra togolana is a relatively understudied species, and further research is needed to fully understand its classification and relationships within its genus.

Its extracts have been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
